scenario: §1.4 DROP COLUMN hero: ALTER TABLE customers DROP COLUMN legacy_segment — 'know what breaks before you DROP COLUMN Oracle PL/SQL'

steps.response: entries=[CUSTOMERS/TABLE VALID, HEROCOL_T_<PID>_CUST_PK/INDEX VALID, PKG_CUSTOMER_REPORT/PACKAGE VALID, PKG_CUSTOMER_REPORT/PACKAGE BODY VALID, PROC_SEGMENT_SUMMARY/PROCEDURE VALID, V_HIGH_VALUE_CUSTOMERS/VIEW VALID], all_valid_before_drop=true

steps.input: SELECT column_name, data_type, data_length, nullable FROM all_tab_columns WHERE owner=HEROCOL_T_<PID> AND table_name=CUSTOMERS
SELECT text FROM all_views WHERE owner=HEROCOL_T_<PID> AND view_name=V_HIGH_VALUE_CUSTOMERS
SELECT name, type, owner FROM all_dependencies WHERE referenced_owner=HEROCOL_T_<PID> AND referenced_name=CUSTOMERS
ALTER TABLE HEROCOL_T_<PID>.CUSTOMERS DROP COLUMN LEGACY_SEGMENT
steps.response: DDL applied; column LEGACY_SEGMENT removed from CUSTOMERS table

steps.input: SELECT object_name, object_type, status FROM all_objects WHERE owner=HEROCOL_T_<PID> AND object_name IN (CUSTOMERS, V_HIGH_VALUE_CUSTOMERS, PKG_CUSTOMER_REPORT, PROC_SEGMENT_SUMMARY)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM all_tab_columns WHERE owner=HEROCOL_T_<PID> AND table_name=CUSTOMERS AND column_name=LEGACY_SEGMENT

what_breaks.reason: Package body references customers.legacy_segment in get_customers_by_segment (WHERE + SELECT), get_segment_summary (GROUP BY), and audit_segment_access (%TYPE anchor + SELECT)
Procedure references customers.legacy_segment via %TYPE anchor and a SELECT in the FOR loop cursor
View SELECT-references customers.legacy_segment directly in select list + WHERE clause; column no longer exists after DROP COLUMN
